_comment: FS-RETIRE-PROLOGUE: the dead Trump/Musk/Kennedy-line prologue events (Friedrich/Kallstadt -> Fred -> 1946 birth, incl. the ev_line_fails -> end_line_failed failure chain) were RETIRED. The live game routes founded runs through the 1776 spine (beginSpine), which renders before any event card, so the prologue narrative never surfaced and only caused early autoPlaythrough line-deaths; its destiny BRANCH on-ramps were rehomed into the spine (spineBranch.ts). KEPT: the surviving life-stage SUCCESSION beats (ev_cp_take_partner -> ev_cp_raise_heirs) that advance the line generation-by-generation on the event path (see founding.ts/effects.ts).

events.title: A Match for the Line
events.scene: The {given_name} who carries the {family_name} name is of an age to marry, and the choice of a partner is the choice of what blood and what alliance will shape the next generation. A union can fuse two houses, lift the family's standing, or simply be a love that asks nothing of the ledger.
events.researchNote: Marriage was the central dynastic instrument of every dynasty, generation after generation — an alliance as much as a bond.

choices.outcome: Two houses become one engine. The dowry and the connections come with expectations — and, in time, a firstborn to carry both names.

choices.text: Marry for love, whatever the rank
choices.outcome: A partner of the heart, not the ledger — the line is happier, if no richer, and a first child soon follows.
Not the richest match nor the fondest — but a mind that will sharpen the whole line, and a first heir to inherit it.

events.title: The Next Generation
events.scene: The {family_name} household fills with children, or does not. How many heirs to raise — and how — sets the shape of the succession to come: a single groomed heir, a brood to choose from, or a careful pair.
events.researchNote: Family size was a dynastic decision with century-long consequences for inheritance and survival, repeated by every generation that meant to endure.

choices.outcome: Everything rests on one child — and so does every hope and every fear.

choices.text: Raise a large brood — let the strongest rise
choices.outcome: A house full of children, rivalries, and possibilities. The succession will be a contest.

choices.text: Raise a careful pair — an heir and a spare
choices.outcome: Two children — one to inherit, one to hold in reserve against the cruelty of fate.
